codeReview.summary: The feature implementation covers the requested MV3 scaffolding, typed background message routing, auth token persistence in chrome.storage, popup login/authenticated flows, and multi-entry build outputs with tests for auth logic, message passing, and token management. I found one reliability gap in popup message transport error handling.

issues.description: sendToBackground awaits chrome.runtime.sendMessage without try/catch or runtime.lastError handling. If the background worker is temporarily unavailable (worker restart/disconnect), the Promise can reject and bubble an uncaught error into popup UI flows instead of returning a typed error envelope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The extension-worker skill procedure requires reading `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/research/chrome-extension-mv3.md`, but this mission run lacks one of those docs in the Relay repo and the transcript skeleton shows no read of the MV3 research doc; nevertheless handoff.skillFeedback reports followedProcedure=true.

Relay library listing: /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/relay/.factory/library contains only user-testing.md. Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 5fb5a569-772c-4e10-a70f-fbfe0df77b77 includes reads for mission.md/validation-contract/AGENTS/features/services but no `.factory/research/chrome-extension-mv3.md` entry.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Repo-root shared state in this environment is inconsistent with mission boundaries: mission guidance targets the Relay repo, while repo-root architecture/services files describe org-planner. This can mislead reviewers/workers when following generic instructions to consult repo-root `.factory/library` and `.factory/services.yaml`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission guidance: /Users/andy/.factory/missions/e9b0e43e-24b4-4baa-87be-b21f151a466c/AGENTS.md says all code lives in /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/relay. Repo-root files read during review (`/Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner/.factory/library/architecture.md` and `/Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner/.factory/services.yaml`) describe org-planner components/ports instead.

summary: Review completed for commit 98c71e532d820d640a34e8b78d1004367b2237a4. Implementation is functionally aligned with the feature contract and passes scrutiny with one non-blocking popup robustness issue plus two actionable shared-state observations.
